basicRepresentations: Status - duly organised and validly existing under applicable laws
Powers - all necessary power to execute and perform
No Violation - does not violate laws, constitutional documents or agreements
Consents - all governmental and other consents obtained
Binding Obligations - legal, valid and binding obligations enforceable in accordance with terms
representations.absenceOfCertainEvents: No Event of Default or Potential Event of Default continuing
representations.absenceOfLitigation: No pending or threatened litigation affecting legality or enforceability

taxProvisions.indemnifiableTax: Any Tax other than a Tax arising from a present or former connection between the jurisdiction of the taxing authority and the recipient
taxProvisions.stampTaxResponsibility: Each party responsible for own Stamp Tax Jurisdiction
taxProvisions.withholdingTaxLiability: Party must pay liability if fails to deduct or withhold
taxProvisions.taxCreditBenefit: Prompt payment of benefit to other party

agreements.paymentOfStampTax: Each party pays own Stamp Tax Jurisdiction taxes

failureToPayOrDeliver.triggerCondition: Failure to make payment or delivery when due

breachOfAgreement.triggerCondition: Failure to comply with any agreement or obligation (other than payment)

creditSupportDefault.description: Failure to comply with Credit Support Document obligations

misrepresentation.description: Representation proves incorrect or misleading in any material respect

triggers: Dissolution (other than pursuant to consolidation, amalgamation or merger)
Insolvency or inability to pay debts as they become due
General assignment, arrangement or composition for benefit of creditors
Bankruptcy or insolvency proceeding instituted or petition presented
Appointment of receiver, administrator, trustee or similar officer
Secured party takes possession of substantially all assets

illegality.description: Performance becomes unlawful due to adoption of or change in applicable law
illegality.affectedParty: Party for which performance is unlawful

taxEvent.description: Party will or likely will be required to pay additional amounts due to tax action or Change in Tax Law

taxEventUponMerger.description: Tax Event arising from merger of party or Credit Support Provider

creditEventUponMerger.description: Material deterioration in creditworthiness following merger

rightToTerminateOnDefault.designationDay: Not earlier than day notice is effective

exceptions: Transfer pursuant to consolidation, amalgamation, or merger

miscellaneous.amendmentRequirement: In writing (including facsimile) and executed by each party

governingLawAndJurisdiction.serviceOfProcess: In manner provided for notices in Section 12

methods: In writing, delivered in person or by courier
Certified or registered mail (airmail if overseas)
notices.restrictions: Notices under Section 5 or 6 cannot be given by facsimile or electronic messaging

definitions.affiliate: Entity controlled by, controlling, or under common control with a person
definitions.changeInTaxLaw: Enactment, promulgation or change in tax treaty, law, regulation or ruling
definitions.consent: Consent, approval, action, authorisation, exemption, notice, filing, registration or exchange control consent
definitions.localBusinessDay: Day when commercial banks are open for business including dealings in foreign exchange
definitions.loss: Total losses and costs determined in good faith
definitions.marketQuotation: Weighted average of quotations from Reference Market-makers
definitions.specifiedTransaction: Rate swap, currency swap, commodity swap, equity swap, cap, floor, collar, forward rate agreement, interest rate option, FX transaction and similar transactions
